p.em: Implementation-grade flow for safe fd lifecycle management

ul.li: Blue Solid: Synchronous Request / Control Flow
Dashed Arrow: Async SQE Submission (Ring Entry)
Purple Solid: CQE Completion / Result (Ring Exit)
Red Stroke: Critical Path / Cancellation Logic
Green Stroke: Final Resource Release / close()

ol.li:
[02] CANCELLING:
Trigger: close_requested
Guard: n_in_flight > 0
Action: SQE Submission (Cancel)

[03] CLOSED:
Trigger: Final CQE (res: -ECANCELED)
Guard: n_in_flight == 0
Action: close(fd), Zero context.

p.strong: The File Descriptor (fd) may ONLY be closed when:

ol.li: Every cancellation CQE has been harvested from the completion ring.
No kernel-side async worker threads are holding struct file *.

p.strong: Failure to follow this sequence leads to:

ul.li: Use-After-Free: Late CQE writes to reused memory buffers.
Data Corruption: A late kernel write targets a REUSED fd in another thread.

ol.li: Thread A: Connection A (fd=5) submits READ.
Kernel: Async READ A finishes, writes data to fd=5.
Result: Data for Conn A overwrites buffers for Conn B.
